target.title: fix(agents): isolate invalid plugin model catalogs [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Isolate generated plugin model catalog load failures so one invalid shard does not clear the whole `ModelRegistry`.
- Keep valid root `models.json` entries and other valid generated plugin catalog entries available while preserving the invalid catalog error via `getError()`.
- Add regression coverage for a bad generated `google` catalog alongside a valid root model and a valid `zai` catalog.

Fixes #92553

AI-assisted: yes, implemented with Codex. I reviewed the changed code and understand the behavior.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts`
  - Result: 8 tests passed.
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts`
  - Result: all matched files use the correct format.
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
  - Result: no whitespace errors.
- Real loader probe with `node --import tsx --input-type=module -` against a temporary agent profile containing root models plus valid and invalid generated plugin catalogs.
  - Result:

```json
{
  "errorMentionsBadCatalog": true,
  "rootModel": "Root Model",
  "validPluginModel": "GLM 5.1",
  "invalidPluginModelPresent": false,
  "modelCount": 2
}
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a generated plugin model catalog with an invalid provider entry no longer aborts the entire custom-model load. Valid root models and other valid generated plugin catalog models remain available, and `ModelRegistry.getError()` still exposes the bad catalog validation error.

Real environment tested: Windows source checkout of `openclaw/openclaw`, Node `v24.15.0`, running the real `ModelRegistry` and `AuthStorage` source modules through `tsx` against a temporary agent profile on disk. No test framework or mocked registry was used for this proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: created a temporary `models.json`, a bad generated `plugins/google/catalog.json` missing `api`, and a valid generated `plugins/zai/catalog.json`; then instantiated `ModelRegistry.create(AuthStorage.inMemory(), tempModelsPath, { pluginMetadataSnapshot })` and inspected `getError()`, `find()`, and `getAll()`.

Evidence after fix: the real loader probe output above shows `errorMentionsBadCatalog: true`, `rootModel: "Root Model"`, `validPluginModel: "GLM 5.1"`, `invalidPluginModelPresent: false`, and `modelCount: 2`.

Observed result after fix: the invalid generated `google-vertex` catalog is skipped, the root model and valid `zai` plugin model are still loaded, and the registry records the validation error instead of silently hiding it.

What was not tested: full gateway/media-generation runtime behavior, a real user install with an existing stale catalog, full `pnpm check`, and full `pnpm test`. `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` was retried but did not fully finish because a few large Windows/platform tarballs timed out; the targeted Vitest, `tsx`, and `oxfmt` dependencies needed for the proof above were available. Local `codex review --base origin/main` was not run because `codex.exe` fails with `Access is denied` in this environment.
target.author: tangtaizong666
